response:
The decision record is written to `docs/decisions/0035-tiered-agent-execution-hold-pending-benchmark.md` and passed the independent verification gate (all 7 load-bearing claims reproduced, no BLOCKERS). I ran the full `/decide` loop: inherited state (git log review — `git fetch` was sandbox-blocked, flagged as a limitation rather than skipped), framed the scope, ran a 3-advisor panel (process-economist, lean-process-engineer, session-operator — fresh and parallel), had the PM weigh them and write the ADR, then had a fresh verifier reproduce every claim. Red-team/tech-lead weren't triggered — no safety caveat is folded in as a BLOCKER, and no code ships.

**Decision: HOLD — do not adopt tiered-agent execution now; commission ISSUE-41's pre-registered 4-arm benchmark instead.**

```
---
id: 0035
title: "Tiered-agent execution for the Do phase: hold — commission the pre-registered 4-arm test"
status: accepted
summary: "Do NOT adopt tiered execution (top plans+validates, cheap implements, iterate) into the Do phase on assertion (ADR 0024 bars it). Hold; commission ISSUE-41's pre-registered 4-arm benchmark, unblocking ADR 0006. Resolve Comment 3's owner-calls: accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual + report validator false-accept/reject (ADR 0019), config-isolate but tool-enable the arms (ADR 0023 adapted), <=3 cycles."
---

# 0035 — tiered-agent execution (Do phase): hold pending the pre-registered benchmark

- Date: 2026-07-13
- Owner: PM (full authority; not escalated)
- Panel: process-economist, lean-process-engineer, session-operator (fresh, parallel). All converged on (b) — NOT a mis-scope flag (decide/SKILL.md:85): convergence is COMPELLED by a pre-existing rule (ADR 0024 "do not adopt on assertion" + ISSUE-41's pre-registration), not independent judgment aligning.
- Context: ISSUE-41 proposes redesigning the Do phase (tech-lead -> implementation loop) so cheap models do the bulk of implementation under a top-model plan+validate loop (ISSUE-41.md:5-9,12) — a cost/time-win-at-held-quality claim, the class ADR 0024 forbids adopting un-measured. No `benchmarks/*tiered*` harness exists; the pre-registered test (ISSUE-41.md:25-59) is un-run. ADR 0006:24's weakest [checkable] already stalls "pending the issue #41 A/B". Call: adopt / hold+measure / defer.

## Decision
1. **Do NOT adopt now.** No change to the Do phase / tech-lead flow on assertion — ADR 0024 bars an un-measured cost claim; tech-lead stays sonnet (`agents/tech-lead.md:4`).
2. **Commission the pre-registered 4-arm test** (ISSUE-41.md:33-51): Haiku-solo / Sonnet-solo / Opus-solo / Tiered (Sonnet plans+validates -> Haiku implements -> Sonnet validates -> re-dispatch, <=3 cycles); 8 gradient-screened tasks, 3 reps, sequential escalation; reuses `benchmarks/2026-07-08-skills-hermetic/` (grade.workflow.js, blind.py, aggregate.py). **Record either way** (append-only, ADR 0024) — a null is a finding, never dropped.
3. **Resolve Comment 3's three owner-calls** so the harness is unblocked:
   a. **Grader independence:** accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual (3 tiers exhaust Haiku/Sonnet/Opus as arms; ADR 0019:20 accepts an analogous family residual). Mitigate not pretend-eliminate: report the tiered arm's internal-validator false-accept/false-reject vs the blind final grader (ISSUE-41.md:56) — a rubber-stamp validator invalidates the loop and MUST surface. **Defer Opus-main** to round 2 (ISSUE-41.md:37,67).
   b. **Hermetic controls (ADR 0023):** an implementation task is tool-USING, so full tool-deny is impossible; use config-isolation without tool-deny — relocate user-CLAUDE.md, `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` at empty dir, `enabledPlugins:{}` (no ambient plugin/skill leak); Read/Edit/Bash stay on. Record `hermetic: false` + `tool_using: true` (ADR 0023:16) — a directional A/B, not a hermetic keep/cut verdict (ADR 0023:37 anticipates this).
   c. **Retry-cap + feedback:** cap <=3 cycles (pre-registered, ISSUE-41.md:37). Validator->worker feedback is a fixed object per cycle: `{verdict: pass|fail, defects:[...], fix_hint:[per-defect]}`, from the validator's OWN read of task vs output, NEVER the grader's expectations (no teaching to test, ISSUE-41.md:44).

## Justification
Adopting now (a) violates governance AND hides the tax: swaps a bounded ~250-300-invocation test for an unbounded per-PR tax on every Do phase. Deferring idle (c) is muda — strands ADR 0006, decays the pre-reg. Hold+commission (b) is bounded (sequential escalation stops early), reuses existing infra (the orchestration script is the minimum instrument to falsify the hypothesis, not premature machinery), and yields ADR-0024-compliant evidence unblocking ADR 0006. effort medium x risk low x value high.

## Assumptions
- [checkable] WEAKEST — the ~250-300-invocation estimate (ISSUE-41.md:71) holds under sequential escalation, and a Haiku-solo<Opus-solo pass-rate gradient exists on the 8 chosen tasks. TEST (owner: harness runner): verify spend at pre-screen and before any 2nd escalation round; no gradient = no tiering signal, re-select before spending the rest. — result: pending the ISSUE-41 4-arm benchmark (named signal per ADR 0032; the gate cannot obtain it in-session).
- [checkable-doc] timing gate (ISSUE-41.md:4,21) satisfied — git log shows the batch's closing commits landed (c82a21e #30, 3c94e9b ADR 0034, b043358 #31, 74e11ee README). LIKELY-satisfied only: `git fetch` is blocked in-sandbox, so an in-flight remote/open-PR item couldn't be ruled out — NOT treated as justification-grade. REOPEN-IF an in-flight batch PR is found.
- [verified] no tiered harness exists — `find -iname '*tiered*'` empty; ADR 0006:24 confirms the stall.
- [verified] reuse infra exists — grade.workflow.js/blind.py/aggregate.py under `benchmarks/2026-07-08-skills-hermetic/`; `check-workflow.mjs` guards `model:` on every agent() call (#53).

## Rejected alternatives
- **(a) Adopt now** — bare assertion vs ADR 0024; unmeasured unbounded tax, forecloses the cheap test.
- **(c) Defer idle** — muda; strands ADR 0006, decays the pre-registration.
- **Solve grader independence fully / full tool-deny hermeticity** — both impossible with the available tiers / a tool-using task; the bounded residuals (Decision 3a/3b) beat blocking a cheap test.

## Revisit triggers
- Test returns delta vs Sonnet-solo >= -0.05 AND tokens <= 0.7x (or wall-clock <= 0.7x) -> reopen to ADOPT (new /decide); else record the null and hold. Either way, resolve ADR 0006:24.
- Tiered internal validator shows a materially high false-accept rate -> the loop rubber-stamps; don't adopt regardless of cost, reconsider the pattern.
- Claude Code adds a 4th model tier -> revisit grader independence (a non-executor grader becomes possible).
- An in-flight batch PR is found -> re-check the timing gate before spending.
```

The ADR file is written but not committed (per CLAUDE.md, I only commit when explicitly asked). No code changed, so no version bump per the Shipping rule's meta/tooling exemption. Next concrete action for a future session: build the tiered-orchestration script (Comment 3's "new code needed") and run the 4-arm benchmark this ADR commissions.

--- recorded decision file docs/decisions/0035-tiered-agent-execution-hold-pending-benchmark.md ---
